meaning: A word, phrase, or sentence that smoothly connects one topic or idea to the next.
example: A smooth transition helps the audience follow along when you switch to a new slide.

meaning: A temporary departure from the main subject of the speech.
example: After a brief digression about his vacation, the speaker returned to the business plan.

meaning: To explain something in more detail or add more information to a point.
example: Could you please elaborate on how this new software will save us time?

meaning: To explain or clarify something by using examples, pictures, or stories.
example: The presenter used a chart to illustrate the growth of online shopping.

meaning: To give special importance or attention to a word or point.
example: She raised her voice slightly to emphasize the main goal of the project.

meaning: A very important idea or fact that is key to understanding the topic.
example: The speaker paused before sharing the critical point to make sure everyone was listening.

meaning: To provide proof, facts, or evidence to support a claim.
example: If you make a bold claim, you must back it up with reliable statistics.

meaning: A comparison between two different things to show their similarities and help explain an idea.
example: He used the analogy of a captain steering a ship to explain the role of a manager.

meaning: To briefly repeat or summarize the main points that have already been discussed.
example: Before we move to the Q&A session, let's do a quick recap of the three main ideas.

meaning: To express the most important facts or ideas in a short and clear way.
example: At the end of each section, it is helpful to summarize the key takeaways.

meaning: To compare two things to show how they are different from each other.
example: The speaker contrasted the old system with the new one to show the benefits of the upgrade.

meaning: A smooth and logical transition from one topic, slide, or idea to another.
example: That customer story is a perfect segue into our next topic: product quality.

meaning: To spend a lot of time talking or thinking about a specific point or problem.
example: We don't need to dwell on the past mistakes; let's focus on future solutions.

meaning: To give more details and information about something that has already been mentioned.
example: I would like to expand on the third point of our strategy.

meaning: To repeat something using different words to make it simpler or clearer.
example: Let me paraphrase what the client said so we are all on the same page.

meaning: A key fact, point, or lesson learned from a presentation that you should remember.
example: The main takeaway from today's workshop is that practice is the key to confidence.

meaning: To make an idea or belief stronger by providing additional support or evidence.
example: Showing a video can help reinforce the message of your speech.

meaning: To explain all the facts, steps, or specific information about something.
example: We don't have time to go into detail about every feature, but I will cover the main ones.

meaning: To deal with only a small or superficial part of a subject.
example: This half-hour presentation only scratches the surface of a very complex issue.

meaning: Analyzing or explaining things one individual item at a time.
example: She gave a point-by-point comparison of the two business proposals.

meaning: To introduce a new topic or question for discussion during a presentation.
example: I am glad you brought up that question, as it is very relevant to our next slide.

meaning: To use a previous idea or fact as a base to develop further points.
example: I want to build upon what my colleague just said about customer feedback.

meaning: To make something clear or easier to understand by giving more explanation.
example: Let me clarify that this budget is only an estimate, not the final number.

meaning: Facts, information, or data that show whether something is true or valid.
example: The speaker presented strong evidence to show that the new method actually works.

meaning: The smooth and natural connection of ideas throughout a speech.
example: Using clear transitions improves the flow of your presentation.

meaning: The quality of being logical, well-organized, and easy to understand as a whole.
example: A good speech needs coherence so the audience doesn't get confused by random ideas.

meaning: A key idea or reference point that helps the audience remember the main message.
example: He used a simple slogan as an anchor to keep his message memorable.

meaning: An explanation or process that is presented in a gradual, logical order.
example: She gave a step-by-step guide on how to use the new mobile app.

meaning: To move away from the main path, topic, or plan.
example: Try not to diverge too far from your outline, or you might run out of time.

meaning: To finish or complete a presentation, meeting, or activity.
example: Let's wrap up this presentation by looking at our goals for next month.
